content.problemStatement:
This problem has subtasks.

Chef has a tree of NNN vertices. Vertex iii has the corresponding value AiA_iAi​.

As a close friend of the chef, he has entrusted you with the task of handling QQQ queries on the tree during your pastime. These queries can be of two types:


Type 111: Given two integers uuu and xxx, set the value of vertex uuu to xxx.

More formally, set AuA_uAu​ to xxx.
Type 222: Given two integers uuu and vvv (where u≠vu \neq vu=v).

Let SSS be the set of all vertices on the shortest path from uuu to vvv.

Print the minimum value of ⌊Ai+Aj2⌋−(Ai & Aj)\displaystyle\left\lfloor \frac{A_i + A_j}{2} \right\rfloor - (A_i\ \&\ A_j)⌊2Ai​+Aj​​⌋−(Ai​ & Aj​), where i≠ji \neq ji=j and i,j∈Si, j \in Si,j∈S.

Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of multiple lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains two space-separated integers NNN and QQQ — the number of vertices of the tree and number of queries, respectively.
The next N−1N - 1N−1 lines represent the edges of the tree. The iii-th of them contains two space-separated integers uiu_iui​ and viv_ivi​, denoting an edge between uiu_iui​ and viv_ivi​.
The next line contains NNN space-separated integers A1,A2,…,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1​,A2​,…,AN​ - the values assigned to the vertices.
The last QQQ lines describe the queries. Each of these QQQ lines will be in one of two forms:

1  u  x1 \;u \;x1ux, represents a query of the first type.
2  u  v2 \;u \;v2uv, represents a query of the second type.






Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line the answer to each query of the second type.
constraints: The sum of NNN and the sum of QQQ over all test cases both won't exceed 10510^5105.
**Subtask 1 (40 points):** The sum of QQQ over all test cases won't exceed 10410^4104.
